questions.question: A 'Golden Cross' occurs when:
options: The 200-day MA crosses above the 50-day MA
The 50-day MA crosses above the 200-day MA
The stock price hits an all-time high
questions.explanation: A Golden Cross is a bullish signal where the 50-day moving average crosses above the 200-day moving average, suggesting a potential long-term uptrend.

questions.question: In a head & shoulders pattern, a bearish reversal is confirmed when:
options: The right shoulder is higher than the left
The head reaches a new high
questions.explanation: The head & shoulders reversal is confirmed when price breaks below the neckline (the line connecting the lows between the shoulders and head).

questions.question: When a support level is broken, it often:
questions.explanation: This is called 'role reversal' — when support is broken, that price level often becomes resistance, and vice versa.

questions.question: Which moving average gives more weight to recent prices?
questions.explanation: The EMA applies a multiplier that gives more weight to recent prices, making it react faster to new price movements compared to the SMA.
